name: create-instance
description: Create a new Minecraft instance with a mod loader via instance(action=create, gameVersion, loader, ...). Use when the user wants to install/set up a new version or modded instance ("装个版本","新建版本","装Fabric","Forge","NeoForge","Quilt").
body:
# Create an instance

`instance(action=create, gameVersion, loader[, loaderVersion, name])`

- Call `search(action=game_versions)` first for REAL, live MC versions — never guess a
  version string from memory.
- Pick loader by intent if unstated: Fabric for Sodium/performance and most mods,
  Forge/NeoForge for big or pre-existing modpacks, Quilt only if asked.
- Check `instance(action=list)` first so you don't create a duplicate.
- `loaderVersion`/`name` are optional — omit for sensible defaults.
- Downloads game + loader files, so it can be slow. Treat as a background job — poll
  with `job(action=check)` instead of assuming it finished instantly.

Don't: pick a loader mismatching what the user/modpack needs; invent a version instead
of calling `search(action=game_versions)`.
